behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the exact string 'Duplicate JSON field name detected while collecting field writers', the merge function signature '(existing, replacement) ->', the class InstanceCoercer, and the bazel build/test commands.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction dictates the exact literal error message and the exact lambda parameter names '(existing, replacement) ->', effectively specifying the code to write rather than just the broken behavior.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on literal '(existing, replacement) ->' substring, so an equivalent fix using '(a, b) ->' or '(existingWriter, newWriter) ->' would fail despite being correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests run bazel build and bazel test subprocesses in addition to text checks, exercising actual compilation and test suites.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require the fix to compile and the existing JSON/core/remote test suites to pass, so a stub cannot satisfy them.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover the required error message, the merge function signature, successful build, and non-regression of existing test suites as described.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings labeling fail_to_pass vs pass_to_pass.

structured_data_schema.reason: No structured data output produced by this task.

no_typos.reason: Paths and commands appear consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction tells the agent exactly which method to edit (the one using Collectors.toMap), the required exact error string, and the exact lambda parameter names — this is pinpoint solution leakage beyond domain-level hints.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, no config edits involved.
